algorithmPage.outputEmpty: Run the code to see output.
algorithmPage.outputNoResult: No output. Add console.log(...) or export a function to run.
algorithmPage.outputTimeout: Execution stopped after 2 seconds. Check for an infinite loop.

backtracking-basics.title: Backtracking Basics
backtracking-basics.meaning: Backtracking Basics is a Foundations technique focused on solutions.
backtracking-basics.problem: Backtracking Basics explores a decision tree by choosing, recursing, and undoing the choice before trying the next option.
backtracking-basics.concept: Backtracking Basics explores a decision tree by choosing, recursing, and undoing the choice before trying the next option.
backtracking-basics.logicSummary: Handle the base case, loop through valid choices, add one choice, recurse, then remove that choice.
backtracking-basics.transitionSummary: The transition is choose -> explore -> unchoose, which keeps sibling branches isolated.
backtracking-basics.codeInsight: The backtrack step is what keeps the state correct: every push must have a matching pop.
backtracking-basics.realLifeExample: Backtracking Basics appears when the input is choices and the required result is solutions.
backtracking-basics.whenToUse: Use Backtracking Basics when a problem matches the Foundations pattern and the expected state changes match a recursion tree dry run.
backtracking-basics.memoryTrick: Backtracking Basics: name the input, state, answer, and stop condition before writing the loop.
backtracking-basics.visualizerCaption: A recursion tree walkthrough showing Backtracking Basics' input, state, transition, and answer.

logicSteps.title: Read the input
logicSteps.text: Identify the exact data Backtracking Basics receives and what output is required.

logicSteps.title: Initialize state
logicSteps.text: Prepare the working variables used only by Backtracking Basics.

logicSteps.title: Apply the transition
logicSteps.text: Run the recursion tree transition and update the algorithm-specific state.

logicSteps.title: Return the answer
logicSteps.text: Stop at the correct condition and return the tracked result.

variables.name: choices
variables.purpose: The concrete data Backtracking Basics receives before any state changes begin.

variables.name: path
variables.purpose: The partial decision path being built by the recursive search.

variables.name: solutions
variables.purpose: All valid paths or the final recursive value requested by the problem.

variables.name: base case or invalid choice
variables.purpose: Stop when the path is complete, invalid, or ready to be recorded.

dryRun.label: Input
dryRun.title: Read the problem data
dryRun.note: Read the Backtracking Basics input and decide what result must be produced.
dryRun.codeInsight: Line 7 normalizes the input. If choices is not an array, the function uses an empty list so the rest of the code can iterate safely.

dryRun.label: State
dryRun.title: Initialize working state
dryRun.note: Set up the state variables that Backtracking Basics changes during the dry run.
dryRun.codeInsight: Lines 8 and 9 create separate state: result stores completed paths, while path tracks the current recursive choice trail.

dryRun.label: Transition
dryRun.title: Run the core step
dryRun.note: Apply one recursion tree transition for Backtracking Basics.
dryRun.codeInsight: Lines 17-19 are the choose, explore, unchoose cycle: push the current value, recurse with it included, then pop to restore the path.

dryRun.label: Answer
dryRun.title: Return the result
dryRun.note: Return the final Backtracking Basics answer from the tracked state.
dryRun.codeInsight: Line 23 returns the collected result after backtrack(0) has explored every include and exclude branch.

complexity.time: O(n) for the educational reference implementation.
complexity.space: O(n) for tracked state when needed.

quiz.question: Which state choice keeps Backtracking Basics correct?

options.text: Track path and update it only through Backtracking Basics' transition.
Reuse a different algorithm's state names even when the transition is different.
Return before checking the algorithm-specific stop condition.

quiz.correctText: Correct. Backtracking Basics stays understandable when its own state and transition drive the answer.
quiz.incorrectText: Not quite. Backtracking Basics needs its own input, state, answer, and condition rather than another algorithm's page structure.
